title: [FIRRTL][Lower-Layers] do not capture uses multiple times
body:
This PR fixes two issues.

First, when a captured value has been replaced by a block-argument on the layerblock, if we encounter a use of the block-argument, we would attempt to "recapture" the argument, because our check for "is this value defined inside the layerblock" was broken for block arguments. This PR fixes that check. Now a value can be captured more than once.

Second, when capturing a value as a "source/input-port", we should only replaces uses where the value is used as a source. Similarly, when a value is captured as a "sink/output-port", we should only replace uses where the value is used as the destination of a connect-like operation.

This means that if a value is used as both a source and a sink, we will create both an input and an output port. In this case, we may eventually want to create only a single output output port.

Fixes: #6694
